perturbation.original_text: A. CONSULTANT'S "ENDORSEMENT" means the right to use the CONSULTANT'S name, fame, nickname, autograph, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image in connection with the marketing, advertising, promotion and sale of ADAMS GOLF'S PRODUCT.
perturbation.changed_text: A. CONSULTANT'S "ENDORSEMENT" means the right to use the CONSULTANT'S name, fame, nickname, autograph, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image in connection with the marketing, advertising, promotion and sale of ADAMS GOLF'S Goods.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'PRODUCT' to 'Goods' in the definition of 'ENDORSEMENT' creates a contradiction because 'PRODUCT' is specifically defined in section 2B, while 'Goods' is not, leading to uncertainty about the scope of the endorsement rights. This will contradict section 3, and other parts of the contract where the term PRODUCT is used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT'S OBLIGATIONS 3. CONSULTANT'S ENDORSEMENT CONSULTANT hereby gives and grants to ADAMS GOLF the exclusive right and license to use CONSULTANT'S ENDORSEMENT in connection with the manufacture, sale, distribution, advertising and promotion of PRODUCT in the CONTRACT TERRITORY.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'PRODUCT' to 'Goods' in the definition of 'ENDORSEMENT' creates a contradiction. The original definition links the endorsement to a defined term ('PRODUCT' as defined in section 2B), whereas the changed definition uses 'Goods,' which is undefined. This ambiguity directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which grants the right to use the endorsement in connection with the 'PRODUCT,' creating uncertainty about the scope of the consultant's obligation and Adams Golf's rights. The 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition applies, as the term 'Goods' is used interchangeably without being defined.

perturbation.original_text: 5. EXCLUSIVE USE OF PRODUCT During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY PRODUCT. (It is expressly understood by the parties that CONSULTANT may play [* ****] clubs in the bag other than ADAMS GOLF clubs including, but not limited to, a putter by a manufacturer other than ADAMS GOLF but may not endorse those clubs and/or putter.)
perturbation.changed_text: 5. EXCLUSIVE USE OF Equipment During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY Equipment. (It is expressly understood by the parties that CONSULTANT may play [* ****] clubs in the bag other than ADAMS GOLF clubs including, but not limited to, a putter by a manufacturer other than ADAMS GOLF but may not endorse those clubs and/or putter.)
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'PRODUCT' to 'Equipment' here, while 'MANDATORY PRODUCTS' remains defined in Section 2.C with specific products, creates confusion. Does 'MANDATORY Equipment' mean the same as 'MANDATORY PRODUCTS'? This introduces uncertainty in enforcement, because the contract later refers to mandatory products and not equipment. This change directly contradicts the consultant's specific obligations regarding product use and endorsement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SUPPLY OF PRODUCT A. During the term of this Agreement, ADAMS GOLF shall provide CONSULTANT with sufficient quantities of such MANDATORY PRODUCTS for CONSULTANT'S use as CONSULTANT may reasonably need to fulfill his obligations under this agreement. ADAMS GOLF shall pay all charges in connection with the delivery of MANDATORY PRODUCTS to CONSULTANT.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'PRODUCT' to 'Equipment' introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text specifies 'MANDATORY PRODUCTS', defined elsewhere in the contract (Section 2.C, not provided, but referenced in the explanation). The changed text uses 'MANDATORY Equipment,' creating ambiguity. The contradicted text, 'SUPPLY OF PRODUCT A. During the term of this Agreement, ADAMS GOLF shall provide CONSULTANT with sufficient quantities of such MANDATORY PRODUCTS...' is directly contradicted because ADAMS GOLF is only obligated to provide 'MANDATORY PRODUCTS', not necessarily 'MANDATORY Equipment'. The change leads to confusion about what ADAMS GOLF must supply and what the CONSULTANT is obligated to use, constituting a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: 10. PROMOTION OF PRODUCT During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall use best efforts to wear the headwear and display ADAMS GOLF'S brand name and to demonstrate, discuss and emphasize the newest features of ADAMS GOLF PRODUCT at every opportunity including but not limited to all Senior PGA tour events and promotional and advertising events in which CONSULTANT takes part.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. PROMOTION OF Merchandise During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall use best efforts to wear the headwear and display ADAMS GOLF'S brand name and to demonstrate, discuss and emphasize the newest features of ADAMS GOLF Merchandise at every opportunity including but not limited to all Senior PGA tour events and promotional and advertising events in which CONSULTANT takes part.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'PRODUCT' to 'Merchandise' without defining 'Merchandise' introduces ambiguity, because 'PRODUCT' is a defined term. Does 'Merchandise' include all items covered by the definition of 'PRODUCT', or something different, like branded apparel unrelated to golf equipment? This affects the consultant's promotional obligations and the scope of his required endorsement. This will contradict places in the contract where the term Product is used and not Merchandise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. BEHAVIOR During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT will conduct himself at all times with due regard to public morals and conventions. If the value of CONSULTANT'S ENDORSEMENT is materially reduced or impaired because CONSULTANT: A.committed or shall commit any public act that involves moral turpitude, B.commits or violates any material foreign, U.S., federal, or other applicable state or local law, C.commits any act which brings him into public disrepute, contempt, scandal or ridicule, or which insults or offends the community, D.makes any statements in derogation, in any material respect, of ADAMS GOLF or any of its affiliates or any of their respective PRODUCT or services and such statement is made to the general public or becomes a matter of public knowledge, then at any time after the occurrence of such act, thing or statement, ADAMS GOLF shall have the right, in addition to its other legal and equitable remedies, to immediately terminate this Agreement, by giving written notice to CONSULTANT. ADAMS GOLF must exercise its right of termination within ninety (90) days of its senior management becoming aware of the conduct giving rise to the right of termination.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'PRODUCT' to 'Merchandise' introduces ambiguity because 'PRODUCT' is a defined term. The explanation states that this change can affect the consultant's promotional obligations because it's unclear if 'Merchandise' includes all items covered by the definition of 'PRODUCT'. Clause 12 refers to 'PRODUCT' specifically. If 'Merchandise' does not include all 'PRODUCT' items, then the consultant's behavior related to disreputing 'PRODUCT' might not be covered under 'Merchandise', creating a contradiction regarding the scope of consultant's obligations and Adams Golf's termination rights based on the consultant's behavior.
